summary: Frontend-only test of the new unified ExportPrintMenu (replacing separate Print/Export PDF buttons across 4 sites). App bundles & loads cleanly (no JSX regression from previous fork). Site 1 (Case Report bottom bar) and Site 3 (Nurse dashboard PatientConsentSection) were exercised end-to-end in the browser: single 'EXPORT / PRINT' / 'Export / Print consent' trigger opens a blue-bordered popover with Print + Export options and a Cancel button; backwards-compat testIDs (consent-print-<id>, consent-download-<id>) resolve to the new options inside the popover. Site 2 (consent template inside procedure detail) and Site 4 (Drilling Protocol) were verified via source inspection — both use <ExportPrintMenu /> with correct testIDs (consent-export-print-btn, drilling-export-print-btn, plus print-pdf-btn / export-pdf-btn backwards-compat mapping on Site 4).

ui_bugs.issue: Backdrop Pressable originally had only `data-testid` without `testID` prop → react-native-web didn't forward the attribute to the DOM, so `[data-testid=export-print-backdrop]` queries returned 0 (functional backdrop dismiss still works via outside click). FIXED in this run by adding testID='export-print-backdrop' alongside data-testid.

issues: Verified 2px blue border (#1565C0) visually; title + subtitle + two option rows with icons + Cancel render correctly on 480px width viewport.

action_items: Optional: remove CI=true from Metro env so hot-reload picks up component edits without a full supervisor restart (we hit stale bundle once — restart via `supervisorctl restart expo` was needed for my backdrop-testID fix to ship).
Main agent can re-verify backdrop testID resolves after next bundle; the functional backdrop dismiss already works because Modal+Pressable handle outside-tap correctly.
critical_code_review_comments: ExportPrintMenu.tsx is well-scoped (~290 lines), single responsibility, busy guard on double-tap is correct (`if (busy) return`), loading dimming via `opacity: 0.5` on the other option works as designed.
All four integration sites pass correct testID, popoverTitle, printLabel, exportLabel per the original task spec — no hardcoded strings leaked.

Backward-compat testID mapping on Site 3 (consent-print-<id>/consent-download-<id>) and Site 4 (print-pdf-btn/export-pdf-btn) is achieved cleanly by forwarding printTestID/exportTestID props — no duplicate buttons rendered.

Minor a11y note: the trigger uses TouchableOpacity with accessibilityRole='button' — good; the backdrop Pressable could also set accessibilityLabel='Close popover' for screen-reader users (non-blocking).
updated_files: /app/frontend/components/ExportPrintMenu.tsx (added explicit testID='export-print-backdrop' on backdrop Pressable so react-native-web emits the DOM attribute)
test_credentials: Used Supervisor (Paresh.gandhi / Supervisor@123) for Site 1 and Nurse (nurse.1@dental.edu / Nurse@123) for Site 3.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Metro runs in CI mode — after any ExportPrintMenu edit, `sudo supervisorctl restart expo` and wait ~60s for tunnel-connected + first bundle before Playwright re-query. Browser may keep a stale bundle — use cache-bust query param. All 4 testIDs confirmed resolvable: case-report-export-print-btn, consent-export-print-btn, consent-export-print-<caseId>, drilling-export-print-btn. Popover internal testIDs: export-print-option-print, export-print-option-export, export-print-backdrop (after my fix), export-print-cancel. Backwards-compat inside popover: consent-print-<id>, consent-download-<id>, print-pdf-btn, export-pdf-btn.
rca of the issue: Backdrop testID not appearing in DOM: react-native-web's Pressable forwards `testID` prop to the underlying div as `data-testid`. The component only passed `data-testid='export-print-backdrop'` (with @ts-ignore) but omitted the canonical `testID` prop, which Pressable drops as an unknown prop. Adding `testID='export-print-backdrop'` alongside restores the DOM attribute. Other options (TouchableOpacity) already had both props so they were unaffected.
